q: What is a goroutine in Go?
a: A lightweight thread managed by Go runtime.

q: Difference between array and slice in Go?
a: Array is fixed size, slice is dynamic view over array.

q: What is zero value in Go?
a: Default value assigned to variables (0, false, nil).
Goroutines are scheduled via G-M-P model mapping goroutines to OS threads.
When multiple goroutines access shared memory without synchronization.

q: Design a scalable API in Go.
a: Use stateless services, caching, load balancing, and horizontal scaling.
